desc:
All looks moist down here and a lot of disgusting mud covers the floor. On the
wall, you notice some big iron rings. Against the south wall, some worm-eaten
planks are lying in the mud. As you look to the north you see some tracks on the
floor.
name: Under the Tower

desc:
You enter what seems to be the house of a wealthy person. Looking to the south
you see a huge fireplace with paintings hanging above it. A bookcase covering
most of the eastern wall is filled with books and scrolls of a somewhat antique
nature. Stairs lead up in the north-eastern corner of the room and a door leads
outside to the west.
name: The Illusionist's House

desc:
You are standing in a well lit room, but you can't seem to find the source of
this ambience. There are open books and flasks bubbling with different coloured
liquids everywhere you look around you. As you look around yourself, you begin
to realise how unlearned you really are in the Arcane arts. A small wooden door
leads to the south and behind it you can hear voices chattering away. There are
also stairs leading back down.
name: The Illusionist's Laboratory

desc:
You enter a small library full of books and scrolls written in long forgotten
languages. Apart from that the room is very bare with the only furniture being a
table and two chairs in the centre of the room. A single candle sits on this
table casting shadows on the walls which seem to threaten you in a very uncanny
way.
name: A Small Library

desc:
This tiny room is lined with shelves containing books, scrolls, and parchments
from bygone ages - writings considered too valuable, too irreplaceable, or - too
powerful? - to be left out in public view. Perhaps looking at some of these
books will provide information not commonly known..
name: Hidden Shelves

desc:
You stand in the armoury with all kinds of armours on the wall behind the
counter. You see helmets, shields and chain mails. To the north is a small yard.
There is a sign tucked in between the items on display.
name: The Armoury

desc:
The street ends here in a small yard. The air here is thick and rank making it
difficult to breathe. All around you can feel people watching your every move,
plotting their criminal activities. The house to the east makes the hair on the
back of your neck stand on end without even having to look at it. An evil aura
seems to emanate from it in all directions out of its dark and narrow windows.
name: The Black Moon Street

desc:
You stand on a small path between two old houses. Children use it when playing
together. It connects the two black streets of Tharbad. These being Black Moon
Street to the north and Black Blood Alley to the south.
name: A Small Dark Path

desc:
The road bends east and south here. A narrow, dark building to the east is
trying to look inconspicuous which only draws your attention to it. There is a
door at the front but who can tell what lies behind it. There are streaks of
blood across the walls of the buildings and a small, dark path leading north.
name: The Black Blood Alley

desc:
You enter a medium sized room. The air is filled with the acrid smell of stale
and fresh smoke. Around you are many tables with gamblers sitting playing cards
and many other games of chance. Thoughts enter your mind of maybe joining in on
a game but you soon realise that you probably lose all of your hard earned money
very quickly. A door leads back out to the west.
name: The Games Inn

desc:
The high city wall to your east closely hugs this dark alleyway. All around you
are splatters of blood where the unfortunate denizens of this city have met
their end. This must be one of the seedier parts of town. There is a gate to
your west and the alley continues north and south.
name: The Sharp Knife Alley

desc:
The high, grey city wall blocks further passage to the east as you travel along
this alleyway. The alleyway bends north and west here. There are lots of small
and squalid houses lining the alley as this definitely must be the poorer part
of the city.
name: The Sharp Knife Alley

desc:
You walk in the shadow of the high wall in the north. Behind the houses falling
in ruins you can see the city walls in the south. There is a crossing to the
west and you can go further to the east.
name: The Sharp Knife Alley

desc:
You arrive at a crossroad. In the centre are the City's Gallows, where the
seedier elements of town meet their end. Main Street continues north. To the
south you can see a small marketplace, and there is a small alleyway to the
east.
name: The Cross

desc:
You walk along a large paved street, among the hustle and bustle of everyday
life you catch glimpses of black clad bodies darting around in and out of
buildings and around the crowds. From here you can see the Southern Gate rising
above the heads of passers by to the west and the street continues to the east.
name: Main Street

desc:
A huge wooden gate looms up before you as you enter the Southern gate of the
city. To the west you can see a small hut which must be the guard post. On the
wall beside you, you notice a small placard with some writing on it. Main Street
continues to the east of here hugging the city walls as it goes.
name: Inside the South Gate of Tharbad

desc:
Instinctively you check your purse hanging from your belt. What a relief when
you realise that it is still full. You can't feel safe in this street and you
keep on looking right and left. The road is going west and you see main street
in the north.
name: Master Road

desc:
The road narrows to the west, most likely to a lesser used or prestigious road.
To the north, however, a dominating white archway boasts of the importance of
what must lay beyond. Beneath the intricate column designs and ornate imbedded
metal swords lies a bronzed sign of warning.
name: Master Road

desc:
The alley gets darker and narrower to the north. On the wall to the east you
notice some splatters of blood which drip down to the ground. Obviously someone
met a particularly grisly and unfortunate end here quite recently, probably best
not to hang around here too long in case you meet up with the same fate. To the
east you can see Master Road.
name: Shadows' Street

desc:
The alley continues north and south from here. Visions of murder most foul begin
to assault your senses and you have a great urge to press on to your
destination.
name: Shadows' Street

desc:
You try to walk silently along the wall on the west but your steps are making a
lot of noise on this paved road. Master road is south of here. Main street is
bending north and east.
name: Main Street

desc:
You stand on the large paved road of Tharbad. You notice a small grey building
to the north and a large house with white walls and freshly repainted shutters
to the south. You can go further east and west.
name: Main Street

desc:
The road bends west and south here and a small alleyway leads off to the east.
The houses are fairly well kept here and the walls have been freshly painted in
stark contrast to the alley off to the east. The richer citizens walk around
with their mean looking bodyguards in tow, some stop and look down their noses
at you in disgust.
name: Main Street

desc:
The alley continues east and west here. To the west you can see Main Street.
Large, dark buildings loom above you and make you feel like they are about to
pounce on you. You realise why this alley got its name as you pick out the
blackened blood streaking across the walls.
name: The Black Blood Alley

desc:
You stand on a wide, and well paved road running north and south. Around you
Tharbad city life continues on as normal without paying any heed to you it would
seem. To the east of you a large, impressive, white building rises above your
head. The building has an air of importance to it, in stark contrast to the
grubby grey of the city walls.
name: Main Street

desc:
You are standing on a large road going south and north. There is a high wall to
the west.
name: Main Street

desc:
The alley goes on north and south from here. You notice red marks on the wall to
the east.
name: Shadows' Street

desc:
You are walking along a very dark alley, the perfect place to be mugged or even
brutally slain at night. The alley follows on to the south and leads to Main
Street to the east.
name: Shadows' Street

desc:
You are walking on a very dark alley, the perfect place to be held to ransom at
night. Main street is east of here. The alley leads west and a small yard is
north of here.
name: Shadows' Street

desc:
The main road is going further east and south. A small alley is leading west of
here. At the corner of main street and the alley there are some letters written
on the wall.
name: Main Street

desc:
You are standing on a large, paved road. As you consider the street sweepings
lying almost everywhere, you ask yourself if they have streets' cleaners here
and if they do, how much do they pay them ? The road is going further west and
north. On the sides of the road you see old stone houses with heavy doors and
dark windows.
name: Main Street

desc:
You are standing on a large, paved road. As you consider the street sweepings
lying almost everywhere, you ask yourself if they have streets' cleaners here
and if they do, how much do they pay them ? The road is going on south and
north. A small and dark alley leads east.
name: Main Street

desc:
The alley is dark and narrow. The houses to the north and the south seem to grin
evilly at you and make you shiver uncomfortably. Main street is west of here and
the alley continues to the east from where an awful smell is coming. South of
here you see some burned down ruins, and a small note on the ground.
name: The Black Moon Street

desc:
A disgusting smell is coming from the stable in the north and you have to
concentrate to not puke in the middle of the street. The alley goes further east
and west.
name: The Black Moon Street

desc:
You find yourself on a small bridge of rotten planks crossing a muddy street
section. The planks, half-way sunken in the mud, bounce with an unreasuring plop
as you step onto them. North lie the Old Tower Street following the city walls
and south the bridge leads into the Black Moon Street. A track of foot- prints
leads off to the east through the mud.
name: A Small Bridge

desc:
At first glance it seems that you have entered a cul-de-sac. Dark and foreboding
houses surround you. A sign is hanging above a door to the west. The street
leads back north, but maybe you could force your way down a small path between
the houses to the south.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
The street continues north and south. Lots of rubbish is littered about seeming
to fill up every nook and cranny. To the east you see a large and impressive
house.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
You are walking on a large and strong stone bridge. Under it, you can see the
Greyflood running slowly. It looks dark and polluted from the filth of the city.
name: On the Bridge

desc:
Filthy scraps line the edges and occasionally overflow into centre of this
large, paved road lined with dull stone houses. Shut tight with heavy doors,
these dingy, lifeless homes seem to peer out uninvitingly from behind their
darkened glass windows and closed shutters. In the south, the sound of the
Greyflood is barely audible as it gurgles beneath a narrow stone bridge.
name: Main Street

desc:
You stand in the middle of a large room. Light is coming from the fire on your
left and from several oil lamps hanging from the ceiling. As you enter, all the
customers turn suspiciously at you but they soon return to their drink. A lot of
smoke is floating above the tables in front of you. On your right you notice a
bar with a man behind it, waiting for your orders. There is a door in the north
and an exit east. There is a sign above the bar.
name: The Thirsty Ranger's Inn

desc:
You are standing in a poorly lit passage. To the west you can hear voices and
music. A door leads east back to the street.
name: Entrance of the Thirsty Ranger's Inn

desc:
You are standing in a big stable with many small stalls. The smell coming from
the hay lying on the floor makes you feel sick. Several people are working in
here and you think you can trust them with your mounts or other pets. A stable
man is waiting here to serve you.
name: The Stable

desc:
You are standing on a wide, paved road. The rubbish continues to pile up on
either side of you. The road leads north and south amidst the drab stone houses
which adorn it on either side. You also notice a small side street to the west.
name: Main Street

desc:
You are standing in a small room weakly illuminated. A counter is facing you but
there is no stuff to buy around. On the wall, behind a strong shopkeeper looking
suspiciously at you, a note is hanging. The exit is west.
name: The Grocery

desc:
You are standing on a wide, paved road. Looking about you and seeing rubbish
lying almost everywhere, you begin to ask yourself if there are any street
cleaners in this town, if there are any, do they ever do their job? The road
leads north and south with either side lined with dark and dreary stone houses.
name: Main Street

desc:
The alley is next to the western city wall. There are some marks on it. You can
go north and south.
name: West Alley

desc:
You continue along this small and dark alley with its littered and broken
surface feeling very uneasy, watching your back continuously as if someone were
about to stick a knife in it. To the south you can make out a small square and
the alley continues east.
name: Snake Alley

desc:
You are walking on a small and dark alley. The paved street is quite broken and
covered with mud and rubbish. You don't like the idea of being alone here at
night without any weapon to defend your purse or even your life. The street is
going west and south. A delicious smell of warm bread is coming from the east.
name: Snake Alley

desc:
You are standing inside a small bakery. A sweet scent of freshly baked bread
fills the room. You feel hungry as you look at all these fine breads well
arranged on their shelves. A tidy note is nailed to the wall.
name: The Bakery

desc:
You are standing on a small alley next to the city wall. It goes further to the
north. To the south you see the riverside.
name: West Alley

desc:
The river bank is dirty and pools with stale, muddy water spreads a rotten
stench in the air. Just south of here some ships lies docked at the quay. To the
north lies the West Alley of Tharbad.
name: The River Bank

desc:
You are standing in a small yard. There is a small pile of rusty and broken
weapons in the north-east corner. To the south you see Shadows' street and there
is a small shop to the east.
name: A Small Yard

desc:
You are inside a small weapon shop. The room is completely empty. All you see is
a big counter to the east with an iron door behind it. The exit is west. There
is a sturdy iron sign standing on the counter.
name: The Weapon Shop

desc:
Even though this building is housed away from the major traffic of the city, it
is one of the biggest and most frequented buildings in Tharbad. Some people are
milling about, hiding in shadows as they warily watch you, others practice some
secret skill under the watchful eye of the masters. Looking around, on the walls
you see racks of weapons designed with only one purpose in mind. The mere sight
of these implements of pain and death send shivers down your spine.
name: Thieves Guild

desc:
You enter a small but well lit room, in fact this is the only room in Tharbad
where one may rest and recuperate minding always to close the door behind him.
name: Rest Room

desc:
This is the reception in Tharbad. The desk is a mess, with papers and things
scattered all over it. It has two drawers, one is locked and barred. A single
window in the northern wall lets in enough light to see what you're doing, but
the corners of the room are still very dark. A small door leads into a room just
south of here and a narrow stair leads to the bottom floor of this building.
name: Tharbad's Reception

desc:
You are standing in a long and smoky room. A bar is set against the western wall
and you see a few tables beside the fireplace on your right. The room is
sparsely furnished and the bar looks antique with many carvings covering its
top. There is a small sign above the bar.
name: The Golden Knife Bar

desc:
You are standing in the entrance hall of the Golden Knife Bar. A stair leads up
to the Inn's rooms and the sound of many conversations comes from the south.
name: The Entrance

desc:
You enter a small shop full of racks and shelves. Most of them are empty but
behind the counter, far away from the hands of customers you see all sorts of
trinkets stuffed into a big box. There is an exit to the north leading back onto
Snake's Alley. A carelessly written note lies on the counter.
name: Thieves Shop

desc:
You are walking along a small and dark alley. The paved street is badly cracked
in many places with mud and rubbish littered all about. You begin to become
uncomfortable with the idea of being along here at night without a weapon to
defend your purse or even your life ! The street follows on to the west with
Main Street to the east.
name: Snake Alley

desc:
The alley continues, with the state of the road getting worse and worse and
still covered with mud and rubbish. The alley leads north and east.
name: Snake Alley

desc:
You are standing in the centre of a small square with people jostling around
you, a few children play their street games as well. This place is cleaner than
most of the rest of city and you begin to wonder why. To the south you can hear
voices, Snake Alley is north of here and to the west you can see West Alley and
the coach shed.
name: A Small Square

desc:
You enter a small office with all four walls filled with pigeon holes from floor
to ceiling. Looking closer at these boxes you notice that many are stuffed full
with messages and small packages. Maybe there is something here for you? A door
leads back out to the west.
name: The Mail Office

desc:
You step into a large paved square. The ground here is fit with thick marble
slabs and decorated with bright green ferns. In the centre of the Yard, a
fountain sprays a fine mist into the air, falling softly into a large concrete
pool. To the east, you see an entrance to the Chief of Tharbad's mansion. Master
road is south of here, beyond the Arch.
name: The Inner Yard

desc:
Around you, polished oak panelling travels the length of the walls to the floor,
only giving way to a thick lush carpeting at your feet. Truly regal, this is
where guests of the Chief wait until they can be seen. A few wide stout chairs
stand firmly beneath a deep blue tapestry, inviting you to take a seat. A small
sign hangs over the doorknob.
name: The Waiting Room

desc:
Heavy grey stones cool this room, creating a small breeze as you enter. Thick
tapestries of blue and white line the walls, acting as an insulator for the cold
wintry months. The room is dominated by a long pine table, around which noble
guests might enjoy a feast put on by the Chief. Thick bees-wax candles flicker
from the tables, as if expecting guests to enter at any moment. A cold draft
comes from the eastern wall.
name: Dining Hall

desc:
This abandoned cistern once held rainwater for the inhabitants of this small
palace to drink. Now, plugged to prevent water and chiselled into from the
Dining Hall, it is more a hiding spot for a smart assassin or wary Chief. It is
often said the Chief, like a rabbit, always likes to have an escape or hiding
place, for when things might go awry. Such is the life of a Leader of Thieves.
name: An Abandoned Cistern

desc:
The room you are standing in is spacious and expensively furnished. There is a
big desk in front of you and a comfortable looking armchair behind it. A picture
of the Chief of Tharbad is hanging slightly crooked on the wall behind the
armchair.
name: The Chief of Thieves' Office

desc:
Lit with oily torches, this acrid black-sooted room is filled with papers and
loan notes. A smaller table groans and bends under the strain of books and piles
of papers in the centre of this walk-in vault.
name: The Chief's Vault

desc:
Here the block of buildings along the east side of the Old Tower Street comes to
an abrupt end. Stone stairs, built into the city wall itself, lead down as the
steep slope descends. From down there the sound of flowing water can be heard.
Westwards a small bridge can be seen, made of some wooden planks, it connects
the Old Tower Street with the Black Moon Street, which strolls through southern
Tharbad.
name: Back Alley

desc:
This is the eastern end of Chapman's lane. The massive city wall just a few
steps to the east, prevents further movement in that direction. Southwards the
East Gate of Tharbad can be seen. A long stretched arch spans across the River
Greyflood, which noisily flows westwards. Built into the city wall, some stairs,
made of stone, lead upwards.
name: Bottom of Some Stairs

desc:
The Greyflood flows to the south here and Chapman's Lane runs parallel to it.
Seagulls and rats are the most common inhabitant of this stinking, polluted
riverside. They can be seen squawking and scurrying along the steep slope,
foraging for whatever they can find. Close to the west several wooden docks lead
into the river, providing landing spots for merchant ships.
name: Chapman's Lane

desc:
Several small wooden docks provide spots for merchant ships to moor while they
stop to load and unload goods. Looking up the slope the Old Tower Street to the
west and the Thirsty Rangers' Inn to the north is in plain view. To the
south-east, a small wooden bridge spans the Greyflood.
name: A Landing

desc:
Protected from the elements under the city's wide stone wall, this area serves
as a checkpoint for watercraft entering and exiting Tharbad. Three torches hang
from metal chains fastened to the high, arched walls on either side of a narrow
channel. A constant trickle of sewage pours into the crystal clear water from a
narrow pipe on the southern wall.
name: Tharbad's East Gate

desc:
Sparkling water pushes its way into the city through a large, rusted metal gate
made of closely-spaced iron bars. Noises from the city come through unhindered,
giving an aural glimpse of what lies beyond the high stone wall that looms high
overhead. The Greyflood flows from the northeast.
name: Outside the East Gate of Tharbad

desc:
Situated just west of the city walls and beneath a small wooden bridge, this
stretch of river is exceptionally calm. The murky water that collects here from
the slow current smells of raw sewage. To the northwest, a narrow channel leads
to a set of wooden docks.
name: Under a Small Bridge

desc:
The water from the river mixes with a constant flow of rotten sewage pouring out
of thin metal pipes protruding from nearby buildings. These houses and shops are
built right up to the bank of the Greyflood. There are two bridges off in the
distance to the west and east.
name: The Greyflood

desc:
This room is rather large, but nevertheless appears cramped due to the sheer
number of objects in it. Cabinets with open drawers are located to the far left.
Stacked just beside is a random collection of boxes, barrels, and other
miscellaneous valuables of such various types that you wonder, if they belong to
the owner or represent illicitly garnered booty. On the opposite side of the
chamber a large opening is broken into the wall.
name: A Cluttered Loft

desc:
The cavern walls here are dark, cold and moist, obviously due to the cold and
foully smelling black and brown water that fills this pool from wall to wall.
The smell from the water seems to be stronger in the larger cavern to the west.
A small stream of bubbles rises to the surface from the cold stone below.
name: A Dark and Smelly Pool

desc:
The landing in the middle of the intersection is surrounded by dark, dirty water
that seems to be the source of the awful smell that is filling this place. The
squeaking noises all around make the darkness seem even deeper than it is. On
all sides of the landing a shallow pool extends, except to the south where a
narrow dark tunnel can be seen.
name: A Dark and Rank Landing

desc:
This part of the pool reaches a small platform on the northern wall, which is
covered with an oozing slime which makes your feet stick to the ground. The
entire north wall is also covered in the slime, and the water here is dark-brown
and foully smelling. To the south you can see a wooden landing.
name: A Slimy Platform

desc:
This passage is quite dry and is climbing upwards to the north, possibly on a
way out of the sewers. The walls are jagged, with shards of rock sticking out as
if to catch at the clothing of anybody who passes too close to them. The
southern wall is different from the rest in that it is covered in slime.
name: An Ascending Passage

desc:
This appears to be the end of a southward passage. To the north is a dark and
sombre-looking door with a heavy iron lock on it. The air here is reasonably
clean and there is a slight breeze coming from the direction of the door,
indicating that this might be the way out.
name: An Ascending Passage

desc:
In this small enclosed room with an opening in the ceiling the walls are covered
with old gobelins depicting various scenes of death and murder. Skulls and
bones, apparently the remains of unlucky adventurers, lie all around. Two doors
lead out of the room: a small wooden door in the eastern wall and a heavy iron
door in the southern wall. A set of wooden stairs leads to the opening in the
low ceiling.
name: The Hidden Pit

desc:
Looking around would lead you to believe that no one has ventured into these
sewers for centuries. The walls of the tube are all rusted and sections of the
tube have collapsed completely. Though the damage is not bad enough to stop you
from travelling in either the easterly or westerly directions.
name: The Tube

desc:
If you look carefully you can see thin wisps of steam or smoke rising up from
the sludge at your feet as it winds its way down to some outlet far beyond this
point. As you trudge along in the dark bits of the sewer walls and ceiling flake
off and land on you soiling your clothes and body. From this point the tube
continues east and west.
name: The Tube

desc:
You drop down into a large, cylindrical tube. The first thing that hits you is
the awful stench coming from whatever is crawling its way through the tube. The
walls are almost perfectly smooth and made of some metal that has rusted away
long ago. The tube continues east and west here with a room above you through a
small hole in the ceiling.
name: The Tube

desc:
This part of the tube has been destroyed by an earthquake or a cave-in. It is
impossible to continue your journey any further west because large boulders
block the way. There is a small niche in the wall.
name: The Tube

desc:
You are down the main pit of the old sewers, but you are not sure that the first
use of all this part is to carry out sewage from the city! You can go up or
south.
name: Down the Pit

desc:
You are in a passage leading north and west. You feel that you are not the only
one down here. As you look at the ground you notice some footsteps in the dust.
name: A Passage

desc:
You are in a small, dank cave and a lot of broken bottles lie on the floor. The
walls are covered by some sort of moisture. There are stairs leading up and a
way west.
name: The Cave

desc:
The ceiling of the passage is low and drips with moisture. Set deeply in the
brick walls there are three doors: a red one to the north, a green one to the
west and a black one to the south. To the east the passage opens up a little.
name: The Intersection

desc:
As you peer into the darkness all about you all you can see are the dark and
gloomy walls of the tunnel. On closer inspection it becomes apparent that this
tunnel was built in a great hurry. The surrounding air is damp and musty, which
altogether makes it very unpleasant to breathe. To the south the tunnel
continues.
name: A Damp and Murky Tunnel

desc:
You begin to wonder if any type of light could penetrate this darkness. The air
is also getting staler and staler as you continue your journey. On the wall to
your left you notice some crude scribblings. Looking around you notice an old
beam across the ceiling with a rope dangling from it providing a way down.
Otherwise the tunnel runs west.
name: A Damp and Murky Tunnel

desc:
This small cave contains a pool of water. The water here is stale and not fit
for drinking and one can only wonder if anything could survive in it. The cave
walls are dark grey, with water running down them making them look even darker.
Darkness conceals the sources of many noises, some of them apparently made by
something living. A tunnel begins to the south.
name: A Dark and Murky Pool

desc:
As you trudge along this tunnel it dawns on you that it was built very quickly
and abandoned soon after that. Bits of ceiling keep falling on your head along
with the odd drop of water. The tunnel continues east and west.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is very wet and slippery here because of the water dripping from the
ceiling and running down the walls. The state of the tunnel is also much worse
than the one you entered into. The tunnel continues east and west.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
The floor cracks and groans, suddenly giving way beneath your feet. Sailing down
a chute of water, swirling around you faster and faster it is clear why this
tunnel was abandoned. Looking sharply for something to grab a hold of, fragments
of torn clothing on jagged rocks dance around your view.
name: A Watery Chute

desc:
As you come round after your fall the first thing you notice is the taste of
blood in your mouth. After that you realise you must have fallen into this cave.
Looking around in the gloom you can see lots of skeletons. On closer inspection
you notice that these are of many different races. You sit yourself down and
wonder if you shall have the same fate as them.
name: A Dark and Damp Cave

desc:
Water fills a narrow passage that opens into a larger cavern to the east. A
disgusting smell flows from the direction of the large cavern. The walls here
are dark, cold and moist, and there is no ledge or landing onto which one could
climb out of the cold water. It is hard to see anything in the murky pool, but
one thing is certain: something else lives here.
name: A Dark and Smelly Pool

desc:
The smell rising up from the sewage starts to remove the linings from your nose
and throat. The tube you are standing in is about six feet in diameter and his
half filled with sewage which seems to crawl its way instead of run. The dark
side of this cylinder loom up as the tube bends south and west here.
name: The Tube

desc:
You are at the end of the tube, here the sewage forms a small pool before slowly
seeping away through some hidden exit. From the ceiling another small tube
enters this one, but you seem to be too large to enter it. The southern wall has
been finished by a wall of bricks making it impossible to carry on.
name: End of the Tube

desc:
Here the thieves come together to plan their dastardly deeds, raids on other
towns and villages, smuggling goods up and down the Greenway. On a large table
some tankards have been forgotten. There are a few chairs set casually around
the table. On the east wall there is a map. The only exit is north.
name: Assembly Room

desc:
It seems you are in a cul-de-sac! There are red paintings on the walls
describing a bloody fight. This is a warrior fighting an awful monster that you
can't recognise. The only exit is west in a red room.
name: The Red Way

desc:
You enter a room which has been painted completely in red. Across the walls
there are many paintings of men clad in black and in various poses. There is a
corridor leading north and another room to the west.
name: A Room Daubed in Red

desc:
The walls of this room are covered in paintings too but, these paintings depict
scenes of horrific mass murder. The paintings begin to make you wonder how they
got the walls of this room so red.
name: Another Room Daubed in Red

desc:
You are in a corridor leading north and south. The walls are all painted red and
on them hang many paintings of foul beasts being slain by men clad in black from
head to toe wielding some magical looking daggers.
name: A Corridor Daubed in Red

desc:
It seems that all of this part of the sewers has been painted red, the reason
for this escapes you. In this room, a painting describing a beautiful landscape
coves all the available wall space. You don't recognise this area of Arda but
you feel very oppressed by these paintings.
name: End of the Corridor Daubed in Red

desc:
After having gone through a small tunnel, you arrive in a small dark room. The
tables are barrels, the chairs were cut from trunks. Some candles on the tables
glow with a yellow shadowed light. Some people are here, all the conversation
has stopped and a couple of brightening eyes have frozen your soul. This place
could be the meeting point of some pirates. Some of the people have a patch on
one eye, and all have a sharpened knife worn at belt. After a moment the
conversation starts again...in whispers.
name: The Shadow Crypt

desc:
This is the secret guild of Tharbad. The walls are painted in red with torches
fastened on them. The exit is west.
name: The Guild of Assassins

desc:
You descend down a flight of old and rickety stairs to arrive at this point.
Looking up you can see some faint light coming from the bar above. To the north
a tunnel leads off into the darkness. The sides of the tunnel are smooth and a
small stream of dirty water flows at your feet.
name: Entrance of the Sewer

desc:
You are in a tunnel that is going right north. The passage seems to be very
ancient. You see some light coming from the south, but to the north it is dark.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
You are in a junction that allows you to go in four directions. To the west, you
hear something like falling water. To the east is a corridor which seems to be
in a very bad shape. The tunnel continues north and south.
name: The Junction

desc:
You are in a tunnel leading north and south. The air is fresh here as well as
cold. Looking on the walls around, you see splatters of dried blood. The blood
probably belongs to some long forgotten adventurer who dared to come down here
to make his fortune.
name: Tunnel

desc:
You are standing over a large, gaping hole, it's very dark down there. If you
listen closely your can hear screams rising out of the darkness. There are
stairs to your left leading down into the darkness.
name: The Pit

desc:
You stand in a small kitchen. There is table on the corner and chairs nearby.
Many tracks can be seen in the dust on the stone floor. There is an exit to the
east and a door to the south.
name: The Kitchen

desc:
You are in a sleeping room. There are some beds made of wood and a table.
Everything is so quiet here, you start to shiver and feel like going away. You
can go west to the kitchen.
name: Sleeping Room

desc:
You enter a small room which is filled with what must be all the Chief's loot
from all of the dodgy dealings that he must of had up until now.
name: The Treasure Room

desc:
You are in an old secret sector! You see a table and chairs around it. There is
a bed on the corner of the room and a lot of dust is covering everything. You
notice some tracks leading north and stairs leading down.
name: The Guarding Room

desc:
You stand in a corridor, which is in poor condition. Rocks are scattered on the
floor, making it hazardous to move about. Along with cracks lining the walls are
some scratches, which catch your attention.
name: The Corridor

desc:
As you advance down this corridor, you can see the state of the tunnel get
steadily worse and worse. It would not be a good idea to continue your journey
east.
name: The Corridor

desc:
This corridor has collapsed. You can go no further, due to the stones, earth,
and wooden beams that block your path. A pool of blood seeps out from the pile
of rocks...
name: End of the Corridor

desc:
Big blocks of rock are blocking your way further west. A noise of water is
coming from behind the stones. The only exit seems to be east.
name: The West Corridor

desc:
The river's water flows in slow, fitful eddies here; small fish dart about. The
bottom is rocky, with mud and debris settled in the irregularities. Old bits of
rusted metal, arrowheads, bolts, buckles and the like stick up from the mud.
Little or nothing seems to have retained any value.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The large flat rocks that cover the bottom here grow into irregular boulders
further west. The current tugs you in that direction, dangerously fast.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The water is still.. The current in the river doesn't seem to affect it down
here at this side, making the soil washed here by the river form a soft,
swamp-like bottom. The dirt that is floating around here keeps the light from
the surface out so it looks dead and calm down here.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The bottom slants slowly upwards to the north, as this is close to the north
bank of the river. The water is calm and the bottom is very muddy, with old
waterlogged trees and debris having come to rest here.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The river keeps its fast pace with a swift southwestward current. Hard mud
covers the bottom, occasionally permeated by brown seaweed. A wrecked boat hosts
a few shy fish seeking cover in its hull.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
OUCH! The current here is swift and turbulent, smashing you against large
boulders. It is almost impossible to keep any sense of direction in the fierce
river, since the water is full of bubbles and dirt.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The bottom is rocky and devoid of loose soil here, as the swift current sweeps
it away south. You have trouble holding on to the featureless rock. To the
south, there is a booming reverberation through the water.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
This is a rather deep section of the river. The water is dirty and blocks most
of the light from the surface off. The seaweed covered bottom slopes downward to
the south. The north bank rises abruptly in a rotten bluff of crumbling
limestone.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
It's very dark in here, there are stairs going down to what seems to be a
cellar. The bar is south of here.
name: The Staircase

desc:
You are standing in the cellar of the Inn. A lot of big barrels and kegs are
piled up against the wall to your left. To your right you can see bottles of,
what are probably wine and spirits. Most of them are covered with a thick layer
of dust, but at one end you notice a rack of clean and maybe new bottles The
only visible exit is back up from whence you came.
name: The Cellar

desc:
You are walking next to the western city wall. The alley continues north and
south. To the east you see a small square, while to the west you see the coach
shed.
name: West Alley
